title: Brubb

'''Brubbs''' were [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] bipeds from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Baros]] in the [[Baros system/Legends|Baros system]]. To adapt to life on an arid world with frequent [[Sandstorm/Legends|sandstorms]], the ancestors of the Brubbs evolved thick skins and tough, transparent [[Wikipedia:nictitating membrane|membranes]] over their [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] for protection. Since there was little to hear on Baros other than howling winds, Brubbs were nearly deaf. Brubbs were strong for their size (1.5 to 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall), due to their origins on a high-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] world. Brubbs also had the limited ability to camouflage themselves by changing skin [[Color/Legends|color]], though the color range was only from yellow to a greenish-gray.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 4" />

[[File:CorellianMerchantsGuild-GG9.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A Brubb at a [[Corellian Merchants' Guild/Legends|CMG]] guildhouse]]
Brubbs were a very social people, living in large communal groups called [[Haba (Brubbs)|''habas'']]. Most habas were extended family groups which raised children communally and operated some specialized business together, such as a farm, a [[Mining/Legends|mine]], or even a vacation resort. Certain "university" habas, however, were drawn from the best and brightest of many habas. These groups had technology approaching [[Galactic community|galactic]] standard, especially in the areas of [[Star/Legends|solar]] and wind power. According to [[Obo Rin]]'s ''[[Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy]]'', it was one of these university habas that first made contact with the outside [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], by beaming radio signals which eventually came to the attention of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 4" /> Some evidence exists of earlier Brubb contact with other inhabited worlds, however. In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] in [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] was known as "The [[Blue Brubb]]".<ref name="Episode I: Anakin Skywalker">''[[Episode I: Anakin Skywalker]]''</ref>

Each haba was governed by its ten oldest Brubbs. As [[Gender/Legends|female]] Brubbs had a lower life expectancy, due to the stress of [[Egg/Legends|egg]]-laying, the leaders were almost always male. At the same [[Time/Legends|time]] that the Brubbs were dealing with first contact with [[Alien/Legends|aliens]], many female Brubbs were arguing for an equal share in haba leadership, and protesting by refusing to lay eggs.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 4" />

[[File:Brasck.jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Brasck]]]]
Since Baros was a high-gravity world with few natural resources and low-tech natives, the Brubbs did not see much interstellar trade. Their main commodities were the Brubbs themselves—their strength and toughness made them prized [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] and laborers. [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] employed some Brubbs as guards on [[Khetanna/Legends|''Khetanna'']], his sail barge. Other Brubbs, due to their sociable nature and interesting appearance, became "ornamental" beings hired as retainers for wealthy [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlders]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 4" />
